_source: FireSmart Canada — Protecting Your Home From Wildfire / Home Ignition Zone Assessment workbook (2022 edition). Items curated and grouped for the WildfireIQ Kamloops hub. Point values are our internal impact-per-effort weighting and reflect FireSmart Canada's relative-importance guidance.

actions.title: Clear roof and gutters of needles, leaves, debris
actions.why: Dry organic litter on roofs is the most common ember-ignition site documented in BCWS post-fire investigations. Check after every windstorm.

actions.title: Use Class A fire-rated roofing material
actions.why: Asphalt shingles, metal, clay tile, and concrete tile resist ember ignition. Untreated cedar shakes are the highest-risk surface on any home.

actions.title: No combustibles within 1.5 m of the structure
actions.why: Firewood, propane tanks, bark mulch, patio cushions, recycling bins, and door mats are all ignition catchers. The 1.5 m perimeter is the most defensible band you own.

actions.title: Cover vents with 3 mm non-combustible metal mesh
actions.why: Embers enter attics and crawlspaces through unscreened or plastic-screened vents. 3 mm steel mesh meets BC building code for ember-resistant venting.

actions.title: Enclose open eaves and soffits with non-combustible material
actions.why: Open eaves catch embers in the joint between roof and wall — a notorious failure point in WUI fires.

actions.title: Enclose or screen the underside of decks and balconies
actions.why: Open decks accumulate embers underneath, where they smoulder and ignite the deck boards from below. Solid non-combustible skirting or fine mesh prevents this.

actions.title: Replace single-pane windows with tempered double-pane
actions.why: Radiant heat from a nearby fire shatters single panes long before flame contact, opening the interior to ember entry.

actions.title: Replace combustible doormats with rubber or coir-on-metal
actions.why: A burning welcome mat ignites the door jamb and exterior wall. Cheap fix, big risk reduction.

actions.title: Mow grass to under 10 cm within 10 m
actions.why: Tall cured grass spreads flame fast and low. Surface-fuel continuity is the #1 driver of fire reaching the wall.

actions.title: Space trees so crowns are 3 m+ apart
actions.why: Continuous canopy lets fire run tree to tree. Selectively remove or thin to break the chain.

actions.title: No coniferous (pine, fir, spruce, juniper) within 10 m
actions.why: Conifers carry volatile oils and burn with high intensity. Replace with deciduous species or non-flammable hardscape inside the 10 m ring.

actions.title: Remove ladder fuels (shrubs and branches under trees)
actions.why: Shrubs beneath tree crowns let surface fire climb into the canopy. Maintain a vertical gap of at least 2 m.

actions.title: Move firewood and lumber stacks to ≥ 10 m
actions.why: Stacked wood is a massive ember catcher and slow-burning fuel bed — it sustains fire next to the wall long after a front passes.

actions.title: Keep the 10 m zone lean, clean, and green (irrigated)
actions.why: A well-watered, low, sparse plantings layer dramatically lowers fire intensity reaching the structure.

actions.title: Limb trees to 2 m above ground
actions.why: Removing lower branches breaks the connection between surface fuels and the canopy — a textbook crown-fire prevention measure.

actions.title: Remove dead/down wood and slash piles
actions.why: Dry deadfall ignites readily from embers and produces intense local flames.

actions.title: Thin canopy so trees are 3–6 m apart at the crowns
actions.why: A discontinuous canopy is the single biggest structural predictor of home survival in WUI fires (Cohen 2000, Calkin 2014).

actions.title: Break up large shrub clusters into smaller groups
actions.why: Continuous shrub layers carry fire as fast as grass and produce more intense flame lengths.

actions.title: Selectively thin dense stands and remove dead trees
actions.why: Reduces fireline intensity as a wildfire approaches. Coordinate with neighbours and your local FireSmart rep.

actions.title: Coordinate with neighbours on shared FireSmart treatments
actions.why: Embers travel >1 km. Your neighbour's untreated lot is your exposure — your treated lot is theirs.

actions.title: Reduce surface fuels (deep needle litter, downed logs) in 30–100 m
actions.why: Even at this distance, fuel continuity drives fireline intensity. A patchier fuel bed slows the approach.

actions.title: Pack a 72-hour Grab-and-Go kit
actions.why: BC Emergency Management standard: 4 L water/person/day, non-perishable food, prescriptions, copies of ID + insurance, phone charger, N95 masks for smoke, cash, important documents.

actions.title: Map two evacuation routes + a meet-up point
actions.why: One primary, one backup — both leading away from forested approaches. Practice the drive with every household member.

actions.title: Subscribe to local emergency alerts
actions.why: Kamloops + TNRD push alerts via Voyent Alert and Alertable. BC Wildfire Service on X is the fastest channel for incident updates.

actions.title: Pet / livestock evacuation kit + trailer plan
actions.why: Crates, leashes, medication, vaccination records, photos to identify lost animals, and a pre-arranged hauler for large animals.

actions.title: Two-week prescription buffer + medical info card
actions.why: Sensitive-group household members may be displaced for weeks. A buffer of medications, a list of dosages and allergies, and a contact card for specialists avoids panic during an evac.

actions.title: Outdoor work plan during smoke + heat days
actions.why: Have a written threshold (e.g. AQHI ≥ 7 or smoke visibility < 2 km) at which outdoor tasks pause, plus N95 supply for unavoidable work.

actions.title: Mobility-considered evac plan (transport, charged devices, ramp access)
actions.why: Identify the evac route, transport assistance, and a backup person who can help. Confirm reception centres are accessible.

actions.title: Renter-friendly evac kit + insurance check
actions.why: Even if you can't modify the building, you can keep a kit, document your contents (photo walkthrough), and confirm your renter's insurance covers wildfire displacement.

actions.title: Set up a clean-air room (HEPA / DIY box-fan filter)
actions.why: During wildfire smoke, a single sealed room with HEPA filtration or a DIY box-fan-and-MERV-13 (Corsi-Rosenthal) cube keeps interior PM2.5 dramatically lower.
